summary: Iteration 2 backend testing for MSME Credit Risk Early Warning System. Added 25 new pytest cases on top of the existing 29 to cover Recovery Workflow, Bulk Import, Audit Log, and Auto-Recovery-on-Critical. Final result: 54 passed, 1 skipped (intentional - synthetic critical-risk path; covered by existing-critical-borrower path test). All 13 review items verified against live backend.

minor.issue: Allows admin AND analyst; spec/review_request didn't restrict to admin only. RM correctly forbidden (403). Current behaviour matches code - documenting in case product expectation differs.
Returns 400 for duplicate active case as specified. However, the error message lives in 'detail' (FastAPI standard) - confirmed contract.

When 'assigned_to' is unset (null) the code still writes assigned_to_name=''; works but means audit details string lists 'assigned_to_name' as updated even when only assigned_to was changed. Cosmetic only.

action_items: None blocking. Optional: confirm whether bulk-import should be admin-only (currently admin+analyst).

Optional: in _recompute_and_persist auto-recovery branch, the `prev_category` variable is referenced inside the timeline content - if prev_category isn't defined for very first compute (no prior risk_history), this could raise NameError. Worth a defensive check, though no test hit it.
Optional: PATCH /api/recovery should reject patches when status is already 'resolved' (currently allows further mutations after resolution).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 827 lines - approaching the 700-line guideline threshold. Consider splitting into routers (auth, borrowers, recovery, audit, portfolio, reports) before next iteration.

Recovery auto-creation logic inside _recompute_and_persist references `prev_category` - ensure that variable is always defined (initialise from b.get('risk_category') before the conditional) to avoid NameError on first-ever compute.
Audit-log writes are not transactional with the action they describe; a failure between the action and log_audit() leaves orphan state. Acceptable for now but worth a comment.
RecoveryCase model uses Literal status values but PATCH endpoint also allows transitioning OUT of 'resolved' (e.g. resolved -> open). Consider state-machine validation.
Bulk-import error rows use df.iterrows index; the row number reported (idx+2) assumes header row at line 1 and 0-based index - correct for CSV but XLSX with merged headers could mis-report. Minor.
All endpoints continue to correctly project away _id from Mongo queries; no ObjectId serialization issues found.
RBAC verified: recovery create/patch/timeline allow admin/analyst/rm; audit log is admin-only; bulk-import is admin/analyst.
Audit log resource filter only matches exact `resource` field; consider an additional `action` filter and `user_id` filter for richer admin views.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added TestBulkImport, TestRecovery, TestAuditLog, TestAutoRecoveryOnCritical - 25 new cases)
test_credentials: admin@msme.com/admin123 (pre-seeded), analyst@msme.com/analyst123, rm@msme.com/rm12345 (auto-created/signed-up by the fixtures)
seed_data_creation: Used existing /api/seed (idempotent). New test rows prefixed TEST_ and cleaned up within the run. Synthetic critical-risk borrower path uploads sales/bank/repayment CSV but with current risk-engine weights it does not reach score>=75 from cold start; dedup test using a seeded critical borrower validates both auto-create + no-duplicate logic.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full backend suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py - 54 passing, 1 skipped (TestAutoRecoveryOnCritical::test_auto_recovery_case_created_on_critical is a synthetic best-effort test that skips when CSV uploads don't push score to critical; its sibling test_auto_recovery_no_duplicate_on_existing_critical covers the same spec via seeded data and passes). Use admin@msme.com/admin123 as admin. File-type form-field for repayments upload is 'repayment' (singular) not 'repayments'.
rca of the issue: N/A - no functional issues. One test was initially failing due to a wrong file_type value ('repayments' vs 'repayment'); fixed in the test file.
